title: Putting It All Together
subtitle: Build a project from scratch

sections.content: You've learned all the essential file commands: navigate, create, copy, move, and delete. Time to put them all together in a real scenario.
sections.analogy: This is like your driving test — you've practiced each skill individually, now you'll combine them in one go.
keyPoints: Plan your steps before typing each command
Use `ls` and `pwd` to verify after each step

sections.instruction:
Challenge time. Build a complete project structure using everything you've learned. No step-by-step guidance — just the goal and your skills.

Target structure:

my-portfolio/
  index.html
  about.html
  contact.html
  assets/
    css/
      style.css
      responsive.css
    js/
      app.js
    images/
sections.prompt: Build the full project structure shown above. Use mkdir, touch, and cd as needed.
sections.hint: Start by creating the top-level folder and cd into it
hints: Start by creating the top-level folder: mkdir my-portfolio, then cd my-portfolio
Create all three HTML files with touch: touch index.html about.html contact.html
Create nested folders with mkdir -p assets/css assets/js assets/images
Create the CSS and JS files: touch assets/css/style.css assets/css/responsive.css assets/js/app.js

onSuccess.message:
You just built a project structure from scratch using only the terminal.

Commands you now know:
  pwd — where am I?
  ls — what's here?
  cd — move to a folder
  cd .. — go up one level
  cd ~ — go home
  mkdir — create a folder
  touch — create a file
  rm — delete a file
  rm -r — delete a folder
  cp — copy a file
  mv — move or rename

That's 11 commands. Professional developers use these every single day.

completionMessage: Level 2 complete! You've mastered the essential terminal commands.
